repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Stat entry studies no-solution NPIV projection functionals; closest entries are stat_ate_overlap_decay_v1 and stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap_v1, both different causal targets and different minimax frontiers.

published_prior_art.cite: ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV defines the no-solution least-squares inverse-problem target and debiased inference, not this minimax converse; CaiLow2005AdaptationConfidenceIntervals, CaiLow2006AdaptiveLinearFunctionals, ChenReiss2011RateOptimalityIllPosedEconometrics, and BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR supply adjacent modulus or exact-solution machinery. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24660, https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0503662, https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0602299, https://arxiv.org/abs/0709.2003, https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo entry gives a spectral-cutoff upper theorem for no-solution NPIV projection functionals.

published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR gives dimension-reduction and thresholding upper rates for exact-solution NPIR linear functionals, while ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV gives no-solution Tikhonov learners rather than this cutoff modulus witness. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961, https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24660.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal has polynomial/exponential no-solution NPIV projection-functional phase diagrams.

published_prior_art.cite: ChenReiss2011RateOptimalityIllPosedEconometrics and HallHorowitz2006NonparametricMethodsIV contain analogous mild/severe ill-posedness rate formulae for whole-function NPIV targets; BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR covers exact-solution functional targets, not the no-solution projection-functional modulus. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/0709.2003, https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0603130, https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo Stat entry records this sanity reduction for the proposed no-solution NPIV target.

published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR is the classical exact-solution NPIR linear-functional envelope to which this proposition reduces. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo entry compares Shen et al. first-order Tikhonov rates to a no-solution NPIV projection-functional intrinsic modulus.

published_prior_art.cite: ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV discusses no-solution inference and Tikhonov learners; BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023SourceConditionDR studies source-condition inverse-problem functionals and iterated Tikhonov ideas, but neither proves this intrinsic-modulus gap. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24660, https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.13793.

novelty_flags.label: proposal comparator positioning
novelty_flags.one_line: The drafter handoff explicitly says the required comparator-promise table is missing while the core names strict comparator relations to Shen et al. and several methodological comparators, so the comparator chain is not auditable in the required form.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:gaussian-sequence / thm:modulus-lower / thm:cutoff-upper
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The minimax claims are accepted as reduced Gaussian sequence claims unless a later embedding or asymptotic-equivalence theorem connects them back to the full i.i.d. NPIV experiment.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The lower and upper rates are bound to P_sr, P_poly, and P_exp as reduced Gaussian sequence law classes.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:representer-profile / prop:closed-form-rates
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The sigma-only phase diagram is accepted only as a conditional subclass result under the two-sided representer-profile assumption.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Closed-form polynomial and exponential rates are bound to the stated two-sided psi_j profile subclass.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: def:oracle-cutoff-index / thm:cutoff-upper
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The cutoff estimator is oracle-tuned by m_n^star; adaptation over unknown decay, smoothness, and representer profile is outside the headline claim.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The upper theorem is an oracle minimax-attainment claim, not a data-adaptive estimator claim.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:tikhonov-gap
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The Tikhonov comparison is accepted as conditional on extracting the exact first-order Shen et al. rate and proving the displayed little-o inequality on an explicit parameter region.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Strict Tikhonov suboptimality is bound to subclasses where r_n^star = o(r_n_tik) for the precisely cited Shen et al. first-order rate.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add the missing comparator-promise table with one row per comparator, exact location anchors, and the formal statement that tightens, reduces to, or remains architectural relative to that comparator.
Make conj:tikhonov-gap explicit by substituting the Shen et al. first-order Tikhonov rate and listing the polynomial and exponential parameter regions where r_n^star = o(r_n_tik).
Add a boundary corollary for sparse or zero representer coefficients to show the realized-psi modulus is not merely the two-sided profile phase diagram in disguise.
Add Hall-Horowitz 2006 and Bennett et al. 2022 as secondary comparators, then state why neither covers the no-solution projection-functional modulus.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: the kernel is plausibly field-level and meets novelty_target=field, but the missing comparator-promise table must be patched before Stage 0.
